name: Constantinople
note: Refounded by Constantine 330 CE on site of Byzantion; Ottoman conquest 1453; officially renamed İstanbul 1930

name: Carthage
note: Punic city destroyed 146 BCE; Roman city built atop ruins 49 BCE; Arab destruction 698 CE — modeled as one place

name: Tunis
note: Berber-era settlement that became regional capital after Carthage's destruction

name: Memphis
note: Old Kingdom capital; gradually abandoned after Arab conquest

name: Cairo
note: Founded by Fatimids; succeeds Memphis as regional capital

name: Ctesiphon
note: Parthian and Sasanian capital; abandoned after Arab conquest

name: Baghdad
note: Founded by al-Mansur on virgin ground; succeeds Ctesiphon as regional capital

name: Xianyang
note: Qin capital razed by Xiang Yu; Han founded Chang'an ~10 km south

name: Xi'an
note: Han Chang'an renamed Xi'an under Ming; succeeds Xianyang

name: Beijing
note: Yuan/Ming/Qing palace cores shifted within the same place

name: İzmir
note: Greek Smyrna; continuous occupation through Turkic rename

name: Konya
note: Greek Iconium; continuous occupation through Turkic rename

name: New York
note: Founded as New Amsterdam; renamed New York 1664

name: Buda
note: West-bank royal capital; merged with Pest and Óbuda to form Budapest

name: Pest
note: East-bank commercial city; merged into Budapest 1873

name: Óbuda
note: Roman Aquincum origin; merged into Budapest 1873

name: Budapest
note: Formed by 1873 merger of Buda Pest and Óbuda
